title: Sump Expedition: Porter Load Planning
strategies: next-fit loading: keep filling the current porter load until an item breaks either the weight limit or the K-category limit, then open a new load and never look back (reproduces the evaluator's weak baseline)

first-fit in arrival order: board each item onto the lowest-index existing load that has weight room and can still admit its category, reusing earlier gaps but never reordering the queue or reasoning about which categories should share a load

class-aware decreasing packing: keep same-category items together and pack heaviest-first with first-fit-decreasing / best-fit-decreasing, preferring loads that already carry the item's category so K class-slots are conserved while weight gaps are filled

seeded local search / metaheuristics: start from a class-grouped decreasing layout and relocate/swap items or run perturb-and-reoptimize restarts to squeeze out remaining wasted weight and class-slots
